question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can an unprivileged operator who holds isAuthorized[maker][operator]==true in Midnight call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) to ratify an arbitrary Merkle root the maker never approved, under the precondition that the operator was legitimately authorized for some other purpose (e.g., repay on behalf), triggering take() to succeed on offers the maker never intended to publish, violating the invariant that ratifier approval must bind correct maker intent, causing scoped impact: gate bypass / unauthorized offer fill? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: Midnight.setIsAuthorized + SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified] Can an authorized operator use Midnight.multicall to atomically call setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, maker) and then have attacker call setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true) in the same transaction, under the precondition that operator is authorized for maker, violating the invariant that gates and ratifiers must not be bypassable, causing scoped impact: atomic authorization escalation enabling immediate offer ratification? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: Midnight.setMarketSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, newFee) to raise the settlement fee on an existing market after makers have published offers at a specific tick, under the precondition that offers are live with price close to the old settlement fee, causing take() to revert with SellerIsLiquidatable or produce negative sellerPrice (buyerPrice - settlementFee underflow), violating the invariant that valid offers must remain fillable, causing scoped impact: fund freeze / implicit offer cancellation without maker consent? [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: Midnight.setMarketSettlementFee] Can the feeSetter call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 0, 0) to set the 0-day breakpoint fee to zero, under the precondition that the market is post-maturity (timeToMaturity==0), causing all post-maturity takes to pay zero settlement fee and the protocol to receive zero claimableSettlementFee, violating the invariant that total credit, debt, collateral, fees, and token balances must stay solvent, causing scoped impact: fee underclaim / protocol revenue loss?
